name: Remote PowerShell Session
description: A remote PowerShell session has been established. This can be used for administrative purposes but can also indicate an adversary is remotely interacting with a system. PowerShell Remoting allows commands to be executed on a remote computer and is a common technique used for lateral movement and administration. This detection focuses on the event logs generated when a remote session is created, providing details about the user, source, and destination.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the remote PowerShell session established at `{timestamp}` on the host `{hostname}` from the source IP address `{source_address}`. The session was established by the user `{user_name}`. Determine if this is a legitimate administrative action or a potential malicious activity, such as lateral movement by an attacker. Provide a summary of your findings and recommend immediate mitigation steps, including terminating the session if it is deemed malicious.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The time the remote session was established.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname of the system where the remote session was established.

parameters.name: source_address
parameters.description: The source IP address from which the remote session originated.

parameters.name: user_name
parameters.description: The name of the user who established the remote session.
